mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economic expansion, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns. This is a direct, always-on measure of economic health.
evidence_refs: fact:Employment is a key coincident indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns more than 50% of the time over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates past 2.5%, it triggers expectations of monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve. Higher future discount rates reduce the present value of equities, leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has a dual mandate including price stability, typically targeting ~2% inflation.
failure_condition: In periods where YoY CPI is above 2.5%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a broad-based economic contraction. This joint signal of weakening supply and labor markets precedes sharp declines in corporate earnings and equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:NBER recession dating often relies on coincident indicators like production and employment.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 fails to produce negative 60-day returns on average over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment, accelerating housing starts (HOUST) signal strong consumer confidence and wealth effects, driving broad economic growth and boosting equity markets. The effect is muted or reversed when rates are high.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a highly interest-rate sensitive sector with large economic multipliers.
failure_condition: The correlation between HOUST momentum and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is not statistically significant and positive during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong momentum in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which constitutes the largest part of GDP. This directly translates to higher expected revenues for consumer-facing companies, driving positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approximately 70% of the US economy.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 6-month change in RSAFS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is below 0.05 over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE), as defined by the Sahm Rule indicator, is a highly reliable real-time signal of a recession. This structural shift in the labor market causes a severe re-pricing of risk assets downwards.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month low
evidence_refs: doc:Sahm, C. (2019) 'Direct Stimulus Payments to Individuals'
failure_condition: Following a trigger, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected rise in the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a hawkish monetary policy surprise. This immediately tightens financial conditions and increases the discount rate on equities, causing a negative price reaction over a short horizon.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of slowing real economic activity (INDPRO) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates a stagflationary environment. This scenario is particularly damaging for equities as it compresses corporate profit margins and increases discount rates simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was a prolonged period of poor equity performance.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return during periods meeting the trigger condition is positive over a multi-decade sample.
